title: merremia hirta var. retusa
intro: Merremia hirta var. retusa, also known as the hairy woodrose or merremia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Convolvulaceae, commonly found in tropical regions. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The medicinal properties of Merremia hirta var. retusa include anti-inflammatory and antipyretic effects, which provide relief from pain, reduce fever, and promote overall health and well-being. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
